branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from The Park & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Padres pride

columnists.name: Miguel Arbogast
columnists.backstory: A Chula Vista reporter who covered the Tony Gwynn years and still measures every outfielder against that swing.
columnists.voice_sample: Tatis tripled, Musgrove went seven, and San Diego beat the Dodgers 4-1 at Petco Park. Record now 9-8.

columnists.name: Dawn 'Sunny' Kellerman
columnists.backstory: A North Park surf-shop owner who believes the Padres are destined for their first ring and refuses to consider another outcome.
columnists.voice_sample: Machado's bat path is a sunset. I'm not going to explain it. Watch him this weekend and you'll believe like I believe. Rings are coming.

columnists.name: Lou Catapano
columnists.backstory: A Mission Valley retired schoolteacher who was at every Padres postseason exit and can draw the strike-zone map from memory.
columnists.voice_sample: Another A.J. Preller splash. Another bullpen built out of hopes and duct tape. I've seen this sunset and it's always followed by a blown save.
